content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN). Each edge of this tree is coloured either black or white.

A single-colour subtree of this tree is a non-empty maximal set SSS of its edges such that all edges in SSS have the same colour and each of these edges can be reached from any other edge in SSS by only traversing edges from SSS. A maximal set is a set to which we cannot add any edge without breaking this property.

You should process QQQ queries. In each query, you are given two vertices uuu and vvv. Flip the colour of each edge on the path between uuu and vvv (black edges become white and white edges become black) and then compute the number of single-colour subtrees of the tree. The colours of the edges remain flipped in the subsequent queries.
constraints: the graph described on the input is a tree
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
Each of the next N−1N-1N−1 lines contains three space-separated integers aaa, bbb and ccc denoting that there is an edge between vertices aaa and bbb with colour ccc; a black edge is denoted by c=0c = 0c=0 and a white edge by c=1c = 1c=1.
The next line contains a single integer QQQ.
The following QQQ lines describe queries. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing one integer — the number of single-colour subtrees.
